metadata.description: Comprehensive sector-coupling and conversion technology database aligned with the Open Energy Ontology (OEO). Designed for PyPSA, Calliope, OSeMOSYS, and ADOPTNet0 energy system modeling. Efficiency definitions vary by technology type (see per-technology description).

notes.electrolyzers: efficiency_percent = electrical-to-hydrogen efficiency on LHV basis
notes.fuel_cells: efficiency_percent = net AC electrical efficiency, LHV basis
notes.heat_pumps: efficiency_percent = COP expressed as percentage (e.g., COP 3.5 → 350%). capex_usd_per_kw refers to thermal output kW.
notes.chp: efficiency_percent = total efficiency (electrical + thermal). opex_var_usd_per_mwh based on fuel input.
notes.chemical_processes: efficiency_percent = energy conversion efficiency to product, LHV basis
notes.dac_ccs: efficiency_percent = CO2 capture rate. capex_usd_per_kw = USD per kW of compressor/capture power; preferred metric is USD/tCO2 in description.

technologies.description: Electrolysis using a liquid KOH electrolyte in nickel-based electrodes. Most mature and lowest-cost electrolyzer technology. Lower current density than PEM but robust and well-proven at MW-scale. Efficiency = electrical-to-H2 (LHV). Requires 20–45 bar output pressure.

technologies.description: Electrolysis using a solid polymer membrane as electrolyte. Higher current density, faster dynamic response (0–100% within seconds), and purer H2 output than AWE. Preferred for coupling with variable renewables. Efficiency = electrical-to-H2 (LHV). Output pressure: 30–80 bar.

technologies.description: High-temperature (700–850 °C) solid oxide electrolysis cells consuming both electricity and heat, achieving the highest electrical efficiency of all electrolyzer types. technologies.description: Combustion boilers converting natural gas or biomethane to useful heat (steam or hot water) for space heating, domestic hot water, and industrial process heat. Condensing boilers recover latent heat from flue gas, achieving efficiencies > 100% on NCV (Net Calorific Value) basis. H2-ready boilers can operate on up to 100% hydrogen blends. efficiency_percent = net thermal efficiency (NCV/LHV basis).

technologies.description: Thermochemical conversion of solid biomass (wood chips, agricultural residues, MSW) into a combustible syngas (producer gas) containing H2, CO, CH4, CO2, and N2 at 700–1000 °C via partial oxidation. Syngas can be used in gas engines, gas turbines, or further upgraded to biomethane/H2 via clean-up and WGS. Gasification type: fixed-bed downdraft (small), fluidised-bed (large). efficiency_percent = syngas energy out / biomass energy in (LHV cold-gas efficiency).
